ddls:
CREATE TABLE "virtual_machines" (
  "vm_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each virtual machine */,
  "vm_name" TEXT /* Name of the virtual machine */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the VM (e.g., running, stopped) */,
  "cpu_cores" INTEGER /* Number of CPU cores allocated to the VM */,
  "memory_gb" REAL /* Amount of memory (in GB) allocated to the VM */,
  "disk_space_gb" REAL /* Disk space (in GB) allocated to the VM */,
  "data_center_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data center where the VM is hosted */,
  "owner_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who owns the VM */,
  "vm_tier" TEXT /* Tier of the VM (e.g., web, database, machine learning) */,
  "operating_system" TEXT /* Operating system used by the VM */,
  "creation_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the VM was created */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Date and time when the VM was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("vm_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_virtual_machines_owner_id FOREIGN KEY ("owner_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_virtual_machines_data_center_id FOREIGN KEY ("data_center_id") REFERENCES data_centers ("data_center_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_centers" (
  "data_center_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data center */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the data center */,
  "capacity_gb" REAL /* Total storage capacity (in GB) of the data center */,
  "current_usage_gb" REAL /* Current storage usage (in GB) of the data center */,
  "address" TEXT /* Physical address of the data center */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data center manager */,
  "security_measures" TEXT /* Security measures implemented in the data center */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_center_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user in the system (e.g., admin, developer) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the user */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "cpu_usage" (
  "usage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each CPU usage record */,
  "vm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the virtual machine being monitored */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the recorded CPU usage */,
  "cpu_usage_percentage" REAL /* Percentage of CPU usage at the recorded time */,
  "average_usage" REAL /* Average CPU usage over a specific time period */,
  "peak_usage" REAL /* Peak CPU usage over a specific time period */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("usage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_cpu_usage_vm_id FOREIGN KEY ("vm_id") REFERENCES virtual_machines ("vm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "memory_usage" (
  "usage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each memory usage record */,
  "vm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the virtual machine being monitored */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the recorded memory usage */,
  "memory_usage_percentage" REAL /* Percentage of memory usage at the recorded time */,
  "average_usage" REAL /* Average memory usage over a specific time period */,
  "peak_usage" REAL /* Peak memory usage over a specific time period */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("usage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_memory_usage_vm_id FOREIGN KEY ("vm_id") REFERENCES virtual_machines ("vm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "disk_usage" (
  "usage_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each disk usage record */,
  "vm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the virtual machine being monitored */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the recorded disk usage */,
  "disk_usage_percentage" REAL /* Percentage of disk usage at the recorded time */,
  "average_usage" REAL /* Average disk usage over a specific time period */,
  "peak_usage" REAL /* Peak disk usage over a specific time period */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("usage_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_disk_usage_vm_id FOREIGN KEY ("vm_id") REFERENCES virtual_machines ("vm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "vm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the virtual machine triggering the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., CPU, memory, disk) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the alert was generated */,
  "message" TEXT /* Detailed message regarding the alert */,
  "alert_severity" TEXT /* Severity of the alert (e.g., critical, warning, info) */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Whether the alert has been resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_vm_id FOREIGN KEY ("vm_id") REFERENCES virtual_machines ("vm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., admin, user) */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with this role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the role */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vm_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "vm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the virtual machine affected */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user performing the action */,
  "action" TEXT /* Description of the action taken */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the action occurred */,
  "log_level" TEXT /* Log level (e.g., debug, info, warning, error) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the log entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_vm_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_vm_logs_vm_id FOREIGN KEY ("vm_id") REFERENCES virtual_machines ("vm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "service_plans" (
  "plan_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each service plan */,
  "plan_name" TEXT /* Name of the service plan */,
  "cpu_limit" INTEGER /* CPU limit of the plan (in cores) */,
  "memory_limit" INTEGER /* Memory limit of the plan (in GB) */,
  "price" REAL /* Price of the service plan */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the service plan */,
  "supported_os" TEXT /* Supported operating systems for the service plan */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("plan_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "billing" (
  "billing_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each billing entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user being billed */,
  "amount_due" REAL /* Amount due for the billing cycle */,
  "billing_cycle_end" TEXT /* End date of the billing cycle */,
  "payment_status" TEXT /* Payment status (e.g., paid, pending) */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Payment method used by the user */,
  "invoice_date" TEXT /* Date when the invoice was sent to the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("billing_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_billing_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "maintenance_windows" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance window */,
  "vm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the virtual machine undergoing maintenance */,
  "start_time" TEXT /* Start time of the maintenance window */,
  "end_time" TEXT /* End time of the maintenance window */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the maintenance */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the maintenance window */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the maintenance window (e.g., scheduled, in progress, completed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_maintenance_windows_vm_id FOREIGN KEY ("vm_id") REFERENCES virtual_machines ("vm_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "vm_snapshots" (
  "snapshot_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each snapshot */,
  "vm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the virtual machine being snapped */,
  "snapshot_timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the snapshot was taken */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the snapshot (e.g., successful, failed) */,
  "snapshot_type" TEXT /* Type of snapshot (e.g., manual, automatic) */,
  "expiration_date" TEXT /* Expiration date of the snapshot */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("snapshot_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_vm_snapshots_vm_id FOREIGN KEY ("vm_id") REFERENCES virtual_machines ("vm_id")
)
